subquestions.question_text: Find the probability that Lucy picks a fantasy book.
Find the probability that Lucy picks a fantasy book she has already read.
Given that Lucy has already read the selected book, find the probability that it is horror.
Which tree diagram correctly represents two marbles drawn without replacement from 3 blue and 5 green marbles?

options.label: A
options.text: First branches \(B:\frac38,\ G:\frac58\), then denominators stay \(8\).

options.label: C
options.text: First branches \(B:\frac28,\ G:\frac68\), then denominators change to \(7\).

options.label: D
options.text: First branches \(B:\frac38,\ G:\frac58\), then denominators change to \(7\).

subquestions.question_text: Find the probability of drawing two blue marbles.
Find the probability of drawing one blue and one green marble in any order.
Find the probability of drawing two marbles of the same colour.
Find the missing probability of rolling a 5.
Find the probability of rolling an even number.
Given that an even number is rolled, find the probability that it is a 6.
Find the value of \(a\) from the tree diagram.
Write the probability of being left-handed and not having blonde hair in terms of \(b\).
Given that 51% of people have dark hair, find \(b\).
Find \(c\).
Find the probability that a randomly selected student passed their exam.
Given that a student passed, find the probability that the student was sitting Mathematics.
Find the probability that two selected students were both sitting Physics.
Find the probability that four selected students were all sitting Physics.
There are 10 socks total and the probability of drawing two black socks without replacement is \(\frac13\). How many black socks are there?
Find the mean number of hits needed to complete the mini-golf level.
Find the standard deviation of the number of hits.
Find the median number of hits.
Find the interquartile range of the number of hits.
Determine the interval outside of which the number of hits is considered an outlier.
Find the probability that a person takes 4 hits or more.
Given that the first person took 4 hits or more, find the probability that both chosen people finished in 5 hits.
Find the missing probability \(q\) in the probability distribution.
Find the expected value \(E(X)\).
Find the missing value \(a\) in the Venn diagram.
Find the probability that a student prefers non-fiction books.
Find the probability that a student prefers only one book type.
Find the probability that a student prefers exactly two book types.
Find \(P(P\mid N)\).
Find \(P(C\cap P)\).
Find the probability that the first candy is black and the second is yellow.
Find the probability that two candies of the same colour are drawn.
Find \(b\), given that 28% of people visited Milan.
Find \(c\), given that 20% of people visited exactly two cities.
Find \(a\), using the fact that all probabilities sum to 1.
Find the probability of visiting at least one city, but not all three.
Find the probability of not visiting Rome, given that the person visited Milan.
Which completed Venn diagram is consistent with the given subject preferences?

options.label: A
options.text: Maths only \(10\), Physics only \(3\), Chemistry only \(4\), none \(1\).

options.label: B
options.text: Maths only \(4\), Physics only \(10\), Chemistry only \(1\), none \(3\).

options.label: C
options.text: Maths only \(10\), Physics only \(4\), Chemistry only \(1\), none \(3\).

options.label: D
options.text: Maths only \(10\), Physics only \(4\), Chemistry only \(3\), none \(1\).

subquestions.question_text: How many students liked Chemistry but not Physics?
Given that 16 students liked Physics, how many liked Physics only?
How many students did not like any of the three subjects?
Find the probability that a randomly chosen student likes Maths.
Find the probability that a randomly chosen student likes Maths and Chemistry but not Physics.
If 3% of watches are defective, find the probability that a watch is not defective.
Find the probability that two ordered watches both work.
Find the probability that at least one of two watches works.
If the probability of a working watch is \(z\), find the probability that at least one of 5 watches works.
For hamburger weights modelled by a normal distribution, find \(P(X=210)\).
Find the probability that a hamburger weighs more than \(240\text{ g}\).
For 10 hamburgers, find the probability that fewer than 4 weigh more than \(240\text{ g}\).
Find the probability that a randomly selected burger weighs more than \(240\text{ g}\).
Given that a burger weighs more than \(240\text{ g}\), find the probability that it is a cheeseburger.
Find the probability that a randomly selected flour pack is rejected.
Estimate the number of rejected packs in a sample of 100.
Given that a bag is not rejected, find the probability that it has mass greater than \(502\text{ g}\).
Find the value of \(a\) in the probability distribution for die \(H\).
Find \(E(X)\) for die \(H\).
State the range of possible values of \(c\).
Find the range of possible values of \(b\).
Find the range of possible values of \(E(Y)\).
Given \(P(X<Y)=\frac12\), find \(b\).
Show the equation satisfied by \(h\).
Find the valid value of \(h\).
Find \(E(X)\).
Find the median number of hours listened.
Determine the interquartile range.
Which box-and-whisker diagram matches the data?
Find the probability that a selected user listened for more than the median number of hours.
Find the probability distribution for the four spending categories.

options.label: A
options.text: Upgrades \(\frac{12}{25}\), special items \(\frac{6}{25}\), extra lives \(\frac{4}{25}\), saving coins \(\frac{3}{25}\).

options.label: B
options.text: Upgrades \(\frac{6}{25}\), special items \(\frac{12}{25}\), extra lives \(\frac{4}{25}\), saving coins \(\frac{3}{25}\).

options.label: C
options.text: Upgrades \(\frac{12}{25}\), special items \(\frac{4}{25}\), extra lives \(\frac{6}{25}\), saving coins \(\frac{3}{25}\).

options.label: D
options.text: Upgrades \(\frac{4}{25}\), special items \(\frac{6}{25}\), extra lives \(\frac{12}{25}\), saving coins \(\frac{3}{25}\).

subquestions.question_text: For 12 independent purchases, find the probability of exactly 3 purchases on special items.
For 12 independent purchases, find the probability of at least 5 purchases on upgrades.
Determine the expected frequencies for the four categories in 400 purchases.
State the hypotheses for the goodness-of-fit test.

options.label: A
options.text: \(H_0:\) the purchase categories follow the model; \(H_1:\) they do not follow the model.

options.label: B
options.text: \(H_0:\) categories do not follow the model; \(H_1:\) they follow the model.

options.label: C
options.text: \(H_0:\) all categories are equally likely; \(H_1:\) all categories are different.

options.label: D
options.text: \(H_0:\) purchases are independent; \(H_1:\) purchases are dependent.

subquestions.question_text: Find the degrees of freedom for the goodness-of-fit test.
Find the p-value for the goodness-of-fit test.
State the conclusion at the 5% significance level.

options.label: A
options.text: Fail to reject \(H_0\); the model is suitable.

options.label: C
options.text: Accept \(H_0\); the observed values match exactly.

options.label: D
options.text: Reject \(H_0\); there is sufficient evidence that the model is not suitable.
